Copilot CLI BYOK plus local models is great for cost and air-gapped teams. But once every org points the same agent flow at different endpoints, memory and safety get flaky fast. Model choice is easy. Keeping repo context and harness rules stable across sessions is the real work.
I keep hitting this with Rust workspaces where bindgen or proc-macros sit in the hot path. sccache helps less than expected once codegen churns. Have you profiled where p2panda spends compile time?
I ended up scaling vLLM on queue depth plus p95 TTFT instead of GPU util. GPU stayed low while prefill was saturated, so util missed the bad state entirely.

Been thinking about the use of imagination in video games lately, or how I feel there are few and increasingly fewer games that ask the player to use their imagination to engage with them.

This post Kazuya Niinou wrote to accompany Etrian Odyssey often comes to mind so I decided to remake it.

images.alt:
How to use your imagination

When your party stays at the Inn overnight,
what do they eat?

If you ever catch yourself wondering that then
you know you’ve formed an attachment to
your characters.

With Etrian Odyssey’s characters, you only give them a name and a portrait,
so no matter how you think of the  character it’s technically just your imagination.
But even in that case, without your imagination, the character is nothing.

For example, a landsknecht who uses an axe might eat his meat with his bare
hands and no utensils, but one who wields a sword might prefer a knife and
fork at dinner. You might think differently, but... if you can imagine small
details like that, you might find that you enjoy this kind of RPG even more.

The essense is an RPG is using numbers to make calculated decisions, but if
you invest those “numbers” with your own feelings, you can spice up the game
little. Think about this:

In your party of five, three characters are dead. Two of them are alive, but they
only have a couple of HP left and no TP. They’re certain to die in their next
turn, giving you a game over. Number-wise, those characters are useless, but
how do you imagine they feel about that? What kind of people are those 2
characters who are about to die? Try to imagine things like that in the brief
time before your game ends.

Are they a landsknecht and a ronin, who’ll die facing the enemy and laughing?
Is it a protector, ordering the weak medic to run with his last breath?

The game over screen looks the same every time, but in your imagination, it
could play out very differently.

The game itself isn’t that big of a thing; what you imagine for yourself is much
more fun. We hope that the player uses this game as a tool, to create dramatic
and fun situations in your own minds.

—Kazuya Niinou

Enhanced Security Command Center (SCC) Standard, now automatically enabled, offers essential security features for eligible customers without added cost. This SCC Standard democratizes AI protection with a unified dashboard for detecting unprotected AI interactions and offers four baseline AI posture controls. The free tier now includes more than 44 misconfiguration checks based on the GCSE framework, alongside agentless vulnerability scanning and graph-driven risk insights. Data security posture management (DSPM) and Compliance Manager are newly incorporated for data control and compliance. In-context security findings are now integrated into Cloud Hub and other dashboards, enhancing user experience. This foundational security approach aims to accelerate innovation for all users. Users can review security posture through existing cloud dashboards. For advanced security, a 30-day free trial of SCC Premium is available. Further details can be found at Cloud Next 2026.

external.description: QueryData is a newly launched tool designed to translate natural language into accurate database queries. It enables the creation of agentic systems across AlloyDB, Cloud SQL, and Spanner, building upon Google Cloud's established benchmark performance.  Hughes Network Systems has successfully deployed QueryData, showcasing its ability to transform user support with near-perfect accuracy. Agentic systems are evolving, requiring precise data retrieval from databases to execute actions effectively.  QueryData addresses challenges in accuracy, security, and ease of use, crucial for agentic applications. Context engineering, including schema ontology and query blueprints, is fundamental to QueryData achieving high accuracy. Deterministic security is ensured through Parameterized Secure Views, controlling data access for agents. QueryData's API offers easy integration and supports cross-database queries, simplifying agentic workflow development. Developers can enhance the system's accuracy using out-of-the-box context generation, evaluation frameworks, and context optimization.  Examples of applications include customer-facing search engines, internal staffing tools, and multi-agent workflows.  QueryData is available now, and detailed documentation is provided for each supported database platform.
external.title: QueryData helps agents turn natural language into queries for AlloyDB, Cloud SQL and Spanner

In a new article, UMC researchers outline key dimensions for critical appraisal of #AI for rare-event recognition in #pharmacovigilance:

🔹Problem framing and test set design
🔹Prevalence-aware statistical evaluation
🔹Robustness assessment
🔹Integration into human workflows

#OpenAccess below👇

external.description: Many high-stakes artificial intelligence (AI) applications target low-prevalence events, where apparent accuracy can conceal limited real-world value. Relevant AI models range from expert-defined rules and traditional machine learning to generative large language models (LLMs) constrained for classification. As the effort and expertise required to develop modern AI decrease, there is a risk that organizations devote too little time to understanding their limitations and sources of error. We outline key dimensions for critical appraisal of AI in rare-event recognition, including problem framing and test set design, prevalence-aware statistical evaluation, robustness assessment, and integration into human workflows. In addition, we propose an approach to structured case-level examination (SCLE), to complement statistical performance evaluation, and a set of considerations to guide procurement or development of AI models for rare-event recognition. We instantiate the framework in pharmacovigilance, drawing on three studies: rule-based retrieval of pregnancy-related reports, duplicate detection combining machine learning with probabilistic record linkage, and automated redaction of person names using an LLM. We highlight pitfalls specific to the rare-event setting including optimism from unrealistic class balance and lack of difficult positive controls in test sets—and show how cost-sensitive targets align model performance with operational value. While grounded in pharmacovigilance practice, the principles generalize to domains where positives are scarce, and error costs may be asymmetric.
external.title: Critical Appraisal of Artificial Intelligence for Rare-Event Recognition: Principles and Pharmacovigilance Case Studies - Drug Safety

images.alt:
A rabid fox snarls in close-up, teeth bared and saliva visible against a stark, pale background. Above it, bold text reads: 

“In this world he will forever be master over bastards…” with attribution to Mein Kampf. 

The animal appears feral and unstable, evoking power driven by aggression rather than restraint.

This image is paired with reporting on a potential retirement of Samuel Alito and analysis that such a move could allow Donald J. Trump to further reshape the Supreme Court by appointing a younger, ideologically aligned successor. The referenced coverage describes a governing pattern of rewarding personal loyalty and expecting institutional deference, raising concerns about deeper entrenchment of influence within the Court.

The composition frames the moment as one of succession and consolidation: not a break in power, but its extension. The rabid fox operates as a metaphor for that transfer—feral, self-replicating, and embedded within structures meant to contain it.

record.text: Ahora dejemos que Perplexity (más ChatGPT) se luzcan. Un informe generado por esta IA sobre el problema de la fabricación de citas falsas por las IA y como combatir el problema. Creo que es un buen informe, al menos como punto de partida. www.perplexity.ai/search/haz-u...

También es una llamada a la responsabilidad de los autores: si añades referencias generadas por una IA a tu artículo mediante copiar y pegar has actuado como un imbécil. Si no quieres ser un imbécil, tienes que verificar cada referencia, una a una. Y solo puedes citar lo que has leído.

Me queda decir que Perplexity es una IA excelente. Precisamente por eso es importante ilustrar este caso. La excelencia de una IA no evita el problema de las alucinaciones, porque están en el corazón de su modo de funcionamiento.

1) Mulher toca campainha de uma porta; tem uma pequena caixa sob o braço.
2) Um ser com grande cabeça atende, abrindo a porta. A mulher lhe entrega a caixa e diz: “Tome.” - o ser olha, interrogativo.
3) A mulher explica: “É o pouco que sei; e que devo a você.” - o ser entreabre a tampa da caixa e espia lá dentro.

5) A mulher sai de cena. O ser pergunta: “Como vai viver sem isso, agora?” - a mulher responde, fora de cena: “IA.”

record.text:
悪くなる時はほぼ「auroraの干渉を増やそうとしてる時」で、
今、真にそれをやってる感じ。
しかも今回はフィルターが丸っと抜ける、
つまり、FLUXまんま出て来る現象が確認できてる。
今回、たぶん、大失敗したんじゃね？
とか思ってます。

Grokの画像生成での推測、前に書いた事と被るけど
Grokの画像生成のベースはFLUXとauroraです。
FLUXは画像は綺麗だけど、制御が効かない。
auroraは元々が極左系のツールで制御は効きやすいけど、軽量。
・美人は出ない。
・学習データ量が極端に少ない。
・立体把握してない。
・男女や人種を混ぜる。
・背景が極端に偏る。
つまり、aurora単体だと画像生成バックエンドとして使えない。
いいとこ取りをしようとして、ベースでFLUXを使い、スケールアップ+フィルターでオーロラを使ってるらしい。
で、良く成ったり悪く成ったりの波は、auroraの比率調整をやり続けてるからっぽい。

大マジで今のGrokは使えない 
・Grokが俺の言ってる事を理解するのに10ターン当たり前（妄想が入り過ぎて全て斜め上） 
・chatGPTは1～無限（通常は1～3、ただし、ダメな時は全くダメ） 
・Geminiは1～2、3以上かかる事はほぼ無い

external.description:
このX投稿（https://x.com/i/status/2042904113416147255）は、@bitter_tenさんが投稿した「ドイツ語のアルファベットの発音」紹介ツイートを引用したもので、@Wladislao_Ghcbさんが「スペイン語版」として似た形式で補足・追加した内容です。

投稿の全体像
元のドイツ語投稿は、英語式の発音（A=エー、B=ビーなど）と違うドイツ語独特の発音を日本語のカタカナで紹介し、意外性を楽しく伝えるもの。
これに対して「スペイン語も意外と違うよ」というノリで、スペイン語の文字名（アルファベットの呼び方）をカタカナでまとめています。
特に地域差（イベリア半島＝スペイン vs 中南米）や日本語話者にとって馴染みの薄い音をピックアップして説明しています。

各文字の発音の詳細解説・補足
投稿の内容を基に、正確な発音のポイントとなぜそのように書かれているかを補足します（国際音声記号も参考に）。

A: ア  
  スペイン語では「a」と読み、日本語の「ア」に非常に近い開放母音。英語の「エイ」とは全く違う。

B: べ  
  「be」。BとVはスペイン語ではほぼ同じ音（/b/ または /β/）になるのが特徴。

C: θe（イベリア）、セ（中南米）  
  ここが最大のポイント。  
  スペイン本土（カスティーリャ語）：「セ」ではなく「θe」（舌を歯の間に軽く当てて「th」の音）。  
  中南米：「セ」（普通のs音＝seseo）。  
  投稿が地域差を明記しているのは、この差がスペイン語学習者にとって一番のつまずきポイントだからです。

G: へ（擦れたカ行に近い）  
  文字名は「ge」。/xe/ という喉の奥から出す擦れ音（ドイツ語の「ch」やスペイン語のJと同じ）。  
  日本語では「ヘ」や「ゲ」に近いが、強く息を擦らせる感じ。英語の「g」とは全く違う。

J: ホタ（擦れたカ行に近い）  
  「jota」/ˈxota/。Gと同じ喉の擦れ音＋「オタ」。  
  スペイン語のJは常にこの音（例：José＝ホセ）。英語の「ジェイ」とは別物。

Ñ: エニェ  
  スペイン語独自の文字。/ˈeɲe/ で「ニャ」のような口蓋鼻音（canyonの「ny」に近い）。  
  「エニェ」とカタカナで書くのが定番。

V: ウベ（Bと同じ発音）  
  文字名は「uve」/ˈuβe/。Bと同じ音になるため、スペイン語話者はBとVを混同しやすい（スペイン語の有名な特徴）。

W: ウベドブレ  
  「uve doble」または「doble uve」。Wは外来文字なので登場頻度が低く、この名前で呼ばれる。  
  発音は「ウベ・ドブレ」。

X: イクス  
  「equis」/eˈkis/。英語の「エックス」と似ているが、少し「エクス」に近い。

Y: イェ  
  現代スペイン語では「ye」/ˈʝe/ と読む（王立スペイン語アカデミー推奨）。昔は「i griega（イ・グリエガ）」とも呼ばれた。

Z: θeta（イベリア）、セタ（中南米）  
  Cと同じ地域差。スペインでは「θeta」（th音）、中南米では「seta」（s音）。

全体の補足ポイント
投稿は全26文字を網羅していない（特徴的なものだけ抜粋）。他の文字（D=デ、E=エ、H=アチェ、K=カ、L=エレなど）は英語式に近いので省略されています。
スペイン語は地域差が非常に大きい言語です。特にC/Zの「th vs s」と、J/Gの喉音は日本人が最初に驚くポイント。
投稿者は地理・歴史・教育系のアカウントなので、言語の面白さを気軽に共有するスタイルのようです。

要するに「ドイツ語に続いてスペイン語も意外と違うよね！」という親しみやすい言語紹介投稿です。スペイン語学習の入門として非常にわかりやすい内容だと思います！  
もっと詳しい発音（音声例など）が必要でしたら教えてください。

value.text: For people just catching up to the Amanda Ungaro / Paolo Zampolli story (she was an Epstein victim, then the mother of his child; he just had her deported by ICE), here's the bigger context: it was Zampolli who brought the Slovenian model Melania Knauss to the US and introduced her to Trump. 1/

This story is fucking insane:
*A Trump crony pursued a teen model who flew to US on the Epstein plane
*When the relationship went sour as he pursued other women, and she married someone else, the crony set ICE on her. 
*Now she is deported.

images.alt:
Ms. Ungaro was a 17-year-old model when she arrived in New York for the first time in 2002, flying on Mr. Epstein’s plane from Paris with her French agent. She never saw Mr. Epstein again, she said in an interview. But later that year, she met Mr. Zampolli, then 32, at a Manhattan nightclub.

Mr. Zampolli wooed her as a client, she said, persuaded her to move to the United States and began a romantic relationship with her that would last two decades. Mr. Zampolli said this didn’t happen until Ms. Ungaro was 19.

At the tail end of Mr. Trump’s first term, Mr. Zampolli was appointed to the board of the Kennedy Center. He moved to Washington for a short time with Ms. Ungaro and their son.

But by 2023, she was fed up. Mr. Zampolli had made headlines for his boisterous parties filled with younger women, and for passing around explicit text messages between himself and a woman he claimed was a sex worker.

Ms. Ungaro left Mr. Zampolli, moved to Florida and married a doctor from Brazil. Their son went to boarding school and then lived with her, and the former couple fought over custody of him.

Then, last June, Ms. Ungaro and her husband were arrested.
